XNYS.Description: The New York Stock Exchange is the world's largest stock exchange by market capitalization, located on Wall Street in New York City.

XNAS.Description: NASDAQ is an American electronic stock exchange and the second-largest in the world, known for listing major technology companies.

XLON.Description: The London Stock Exchange is one of the world's oldest and largest stock exchanges, and the primary exchange in the United Kingdom.

XPAR.Description: Euronext Paris is the French stock exchange, part of the pan-European Euronext group, and home to the CAC 40 index.

XTKS.Description: The Tokyo Stock Exchange is the largest exchange in Japan and the third-largest in the world by market capitalization.

XSHG.Description: The Shanghai Stock Exchange is one of China's two major exchanges and the third-largest in the world by market capitalization.

XHKG.Description: Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing operates the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, a major gateway for international investment into China.

XTSE.Description: The Toronto Stock Exchange is the largest exchange in Canada and the ninth-largest in the world, strong in mining and energy sectors.

XETR.Description: The Frankfurt Stock Exchange (Xetra) is the largest of Germany's seven stock exchanges and one of the most important in Europe.

XASX.Description: The Australian Securities Exchange is Australia's primary exchange, strong in mining, banking, and commodities sectors.

XBOM.Description: The Bombay Stock Exchange is Asia's oldest stock exchange and one of India's two major exchanges, home to the SENSEX index.

XNSE.Description: The National Stock Exchange of India is India's largest exchange by trading volume and home to the NIFTY 50 benchmark index.

XKRX.Description: The Korea Exchange operates South Korea's securities and derivatives markets, home to the KOSPI index.

XSWX.Description: SIX Swiss Exchange is Switzerland's principal stock exchange, known for listing major pharmaceutical and financial companies.

XJSE.Description: The Johannesburg Stock Exchange is the largest exchange in Africa and among the top 20 globally, strong in mining and resources.

BVMF.Description: B3 (Brasil Bolsa Balcão) is the main stock exchange in Brazil and Latin America's largest, formed by the merger of BM&FBOVESPA and CETIP.

XMEX.Description: The Mexican Stock Exchange (Bolsa Mexicana de Valores) is Mexico's only stock exchange, tracking the IPC index.

XSES.Description: Singapore Exchange is the main stock exchange in Singapore, a key financial hub in Southeast Asia.

XTAI.Description: The Taiwan Stock Exchange is Taiwan's primary securities exchange, heavily weighted toward the semiconductor industry.

XMOS.Description: Moscow Exchange is Russia's largest exchange group, operating trading markets in equities, bonds, derivatives, currencies, and commodities.
